skill.name: commit-work
skill.description: Create focused git commits by reviewing changes, staging only intended work, splitting mixed changes, and writing Conventional Commit messages.
skill.summary: Guide agents through safe, focused git commits with clear review steps and commit messages.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell command and weak cryptography patterns. Review found the weak cryptography hits are false positives, the URL is a public documentation reference, and the command examples are expected git operations for this skill. Risk remains medium because the workflow instructs agents to run git commands that can change repository state and may trigger local hooks or tests.

medium_findings.title: Git Command Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The skill instructs agents to run git commands such as status, diff, add, restore, and commit. These commands are expected for a commit workflow, but they can modify the git index, create commits, or trigger local hooks and verification commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain direct git command instructions and examples. The context confirms these commands are part of the advertised commit workflow, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URL
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE as a security issue. README.md links to the public Conventional Commits specification and does not instruct the agent to send repository data to that site.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a plain documentation reference. No evidence found of network requests, data upload, credential handling, or remote execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Scanner Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged lines describe commit messages, skill goals, and workflow text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain ordinary markdown prose about commits and descriptions. There is no code or command that performs cryptographic hashing or encryption.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository-Mutating Git Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends git add, git restore --staged, and git commit. These are not malicious, but they can change repository state and should be used only after reviewing diffs and staged content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and can alter git state. The surrounding instructions emphasize review and selective staging, which lowers the likelihood of abuse.

content.value_statement: Mixed working trees make code review harder and increase release risk. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused staging, review, and commit messages.
actual_capabilities: Reviews the working tree with git status, git diff, and diff statistics.
Plans whether changes should be one commit or several focused commits.
Uses patch staging guidance for mixed changes inside the same file.
Checks staged changes before committing with git diff --cached.
Writes Conventional Commit messages with clear what and why explanations.
Reports commit messages, summaries, staging commands, and verification results.
limitations: Requires a git repository and local command access.
Can change repository state when staging files or creating commits.
Depends on available project tests, linters, and git hooks.
Does not replace dedicated secret scanning or policy enforcement tools.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reviewable Feature Commits
use_cases.description: Split feature work, tests, and refactors into commits that reviewers can inspect quickly.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Commit Messages
use_cases.description: Apply Conventional Commits format when a repository needs consistent release notes and changelog inputs.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Mixed Local Changes
use_cases.description: Use patch staging to separate unrelated hunks before committing work from a long development session.

prompt_templates.title: Commit My Current Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one safe commit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my working tree, stage only the intended changes, and create a clear Conventional Commit.

prompt_templates.title: Write A Commit Message
prompt_templates.scenario: User has already staged the right files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the staged diff and suggest a Conventional Commit message with a concise body.

prompt_templates.title: Split Mixed Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the current changes, propose logical commit boundaries, and use patch staging where needed.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Release-Ready Commit Series
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this working tree into a sequence of focused commits, run relevant checks, and summarize each commit for review.

output_examples.output: Creates one focused fix commit, explains the token validation change, and reports the staged diff review and checks run.

output_examples.input: Split my formatting, API, and test changes.
output: Second commit contains the API behavior change.
Third commit contains related test coverage.

output_examples.input: I staged files already. Is the commit message good?
output_examples.output: Reviews the staged diff, suggests a Conventional Commit subject and body, and flags any staged unrelated changes.

best_practices: Review unstaged and staged diffs before every commit.
Keep each commit focused on one logical change.
Run the smallest meaningful verification before committing.
anti_patterns: Do not stage the entire working tree without reviewing individual changes.
Do not mix formatting churn with behavior changes in one commit.
Do not commit secrets, debug logs, or unrelated local edits.

faq.question: Does this skill create commits automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide staging and commit creation when the agent has command access. Review the staged diff before approving changes.

faq.question: Does it support Conventional Commits?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow requires Conventional Commit style by default.

faq.question: Can it split one file into multiple commits?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends patch staging when unrelated changes are mixed in one file.

faq.question: Will it run tests?
faq.answer: It asks the agent to run the fastest meaningful project check when available.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: The skill is markdown-only and has no built-in network code. Git commands still need normal repository access.

faq.question: What should I review before committing?
faq.answer: Review git status, unstaged diffs, staged diffs, selected files, commit messages, and verification results.
